mission.title: virtual-kubelet: Feature: Implement Port forwarding
mission.description: Feature: Implement Port forwarding. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current virtual-kubelet deployment
steps.description:
Verify your virtual-kubelet version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=virtual-kubelet
helm list -n virtual-kubelet 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working virtual-kubelet installation.

steps.title: Review virtual-kubelet configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant virtual-kubelet configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=virtual-kubelet
kubectl get configmap -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=virtual-kubelet
```
**Alpha Release**

There's a need to be able to open a port on the host running kubectl and tunnel through to a port on a Pod. We need to implement this capability in VK, which will likely require an update to the Provider interface.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature: Implement Port forwarding
steps.description:
IIUC this is not as trivial as an interface addition but actually requires the VK to support SPDY (which is part of moby/spdystream) so the kube-apiserver can do async bidirectional streaming. SPDY is a deprecated in favor of HTTP/2 but Kubernetes still relies a lot on it and the only other supported alternative is Websockets, which I'm unsure if supports port-forwarding.

So is this something we want to tackle now, soon, later, or just a moonshot?

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=virtual-kubelet
kubectl get events -n virtual-kubelet --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature: Implement Port forwarding" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: IIUC this is not as trivial as an interface addition but actually requires the VK to support SPDY (which is part of moby/spdystream) so the kube-apiserver can do async bidirectional streaming. SPDY is a deprecated in favor of HTTP/2 but Kubernetes still relies a lot on it and the only other supported alternative is Websockets, which I'm unsure if supports port-forwarding.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with virtual-kubelet installed or the issue environment reproducible.
